channel.title: New local problems on FixMyStreet
channel.description: The latest local problems reported by users

item.title: Trolley on the public garden, 17th March
item.description:
Trolley left on the public garden. It may contain whatever harmful for the kids playing in the square

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Battery Road, Thamesmead
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Flytipping in the alleyway, 17th March
item.description:
Flytipping in the alleyway behind 195 Woolwich Road SE2 0PX. The Flytipping is rubble, building materials and household equipment. The tipping is stopping residents having access along the alleyway for skip or delivery Lorrie’s. Children play in the alleyway and are climbing on the pile of rubble which is dangerous.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Amberley Road, West Heath, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Pavement parking, 17th March
item.description:
People from new development are getting in the habit of parking in this bay and on pavement around it because lines and signage not yet in place. Better to deal with issue sooner than later!

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Vauxhall Street, Oval, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Exposed ironwork on the single lane Alma Road, 17th March
item.description:
After the Thames Water burst water main incident there are roadworks and by house 157 on the single lane section, there is now exposed ironwork where the road has broken away. This is highly dangerous for motorists and motor cyclists. Please get this repaired urgently.

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Alma Road, Enfield
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Blocking drive double yellow blips, 17th March
item.description:
Also as the road is narrow and this area is used for passing there is a traffic jam as no one can get by each other. This happens every night between 8-10pm
Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Kyverdale Road, Hackney, London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Flytipping in the alleyway, 17th March
item.description:
Flytipping to the rear of 34 Amberley Road. The residents of number 34 Amberley Road are just throwing Furniture, Paint tins and Household Equipment into the alleyway that runs between Amberley Road and Woolwich Road. This situation has been going on for at least a year. There are now children playing in the dumped flytipped rubbish.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Amberley Road, West Heath, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Faulty drain top. Dangerous and loud., 17th March
item.description:
The drain cover is loose in the middle of the road. As cars go over it clatters around, sounding like it will give way. It’s extremely loud as cars drive over it and there are lots of flats surrounding. The issue is a new one as it’s a result of recent work that has been done on the road
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 271, Wick Road, Homerton, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Dumped car, 17th March
item.description:
Dumped car

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Kirkwood Road, Nunhead, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Dumped car, 17th March
item.description:
Dumped car no tax

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Kirkwood Road, Peckham, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Black paint graffiti on soft flooring of play area by rock climbing, 17th March
item.description:
There is large black painted graffiti on the soft flooring of the children’s play area with swear words which need removing asap please Hopefully not spoiling the soft flooring with hard abrasive which will spoil the flooring .

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Main Road, St Paul's Cray, Orpington, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Hazardous parking, 17th March
item.description:
This van is parked illegally and is hazardous to both pedestrians and motorists as it’s parked on blind spot. An accident is awaiting to happen if large vehicles continue to park on this blind spot. Council should inform driver not to park there and make that area double yellow lines.

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Suffolk Road, Enfield
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Outside no.21, 17th March
item.description:
Street light not working
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 17, Glenmore Road, Belsize Park, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: 50 to 90 Priory Park Road, 17th March
item.description:
Litter all down the pavement which really needs picking up please
Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Priory Park Road, London
Report on FixMyStreet
item.category: Street cleaning - Grass verges / Shrub beds - Littering

item.title: Degraded road with deep pot hole, 17th March
item.description:
At the Junction with waldon road and Elmstead Lane, on the mini roundabout there. There is a big hole opening up on the waldon road side of the roundabout, the road surface around the roundabout and the hole is starting to brake up, leaving loose gravel all over the road. The roundabout markings are also wearing away.
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Elmstead Lane, Elmstead, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Big potholes, 17th March
item.description:
Potholes near zebra crossing

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 272, East Barnet Road, East Barnet, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Fallen tree with banches completely blocking the cycle lane, 17th March
item.description:
The tree is falling more and more. I went under it yesterday but did not notice it in time today and was wipped by it. This will make me sway into the main road on the way to work tomorrow.
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): A21, Bromley Common, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Large mattress dumped on bench, 17th March
item.description:
Hello, please could the mattress flytipped on the bench be removed asap please? It is taking up all the bench and people do use the bench to sit on. Thank you!

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Park Hill Road, Shortlands, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Bromley council leaves bagged up but not collected, 17th March
item.description:
It’s great that the council have cleared the leaves and debris from the kerb but it would be much nicer if the bags could be collected before they are opened by animals and spread along the road again. They’ve been here for almost 2 weeks.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Raymond Road, Elmers End, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: 4 very deep Pot holes, 17th March
item.description:
These pot holes are getting much worse and will likely cause a serious accident to a motor cyclist or other road user soon. These pot holes have been there for several months.
Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Court Road, Eltham
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Dumped rubbish, 17th March
item.description:
By the green box and shrubs at the entrance to Tesco off Croydon Rd near the bus stop

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Elmers End, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet
